planets.name: Mercury
planets.description: Mercury is the smallest and fastest planet in our solar system. It has a heavily cratered surface and experiences extreme temperature variations.
planets.interestingFact: Despite being closest to the Sun, Mercury has ice in permanently shadowed craters at its poles.

planets.name: Venus
planets.description: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to their similar size and composition. It has a thick toxic atmosphere that traps heat, making it the hottest planet.
planets.interestingFact: Venus rotates backwards compared to other planets, meaning the Sun rises in the west and sets in the east.

planets.name: Earth
planets.description: Earth is our home planet and the only known place in the universe to support life. It has liquid water on its surface and a dynamic climate system.
planets.interestingFact: Earth is the only planet not named after a Roman or Greek god.

planets.name: Mars
planets.description: Mars is known as the 'Red Planet' due to the iron oxide (rust) on its surface. It has the tallest volcano in the solar system, Olympus Mons.
planets.interestingFact: Mars has seasons like Earth, but they last twice as long because its year is nearly double Earth's.

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.description: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system, more than twice as massive as all other planets combined. It has a Great Red Spot, a giant storm that has been raging for centuries.
planets.interestingFact: Jupiter has faint rings, but they're not as visible as Saturn's.

planets.name: Saturn
planets.description: Saturn is famous for its magnificent ring system, made primarily of ice particles with a smaller amount of rocky debris and dust.
planets.interestingFact: Saturn is less dense than water, meaning it would float if there were a bathtub big enough to hold it.

planets.name: Uranus
planets.description: Uranus is an ice giant with a unique feature—it rotates on its side. The planet appears to roll through space rather than spin like a top.
planets.interestingFact: Uranus was the first planet discovered with a telescope (by William Herschel in 1781).

planets.name: Neptune
planets.description: Neptune is the windiest planet in the solar system, with winds reaching up to 2,100 km/h (1,300 mph). It's a dark, cold ice giant.
planets.interestingFact: Neptune was the first planet located through mathematical predictions rather than observation.
